question_text: Which of these is NOT a way to increase the strength of an electromagnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Add more coils of wire around the iron core

options.label: B
options.text: Increase the current through the wire

options.label: C
options.text: Use a plastic core instead of an iron core

correct_answer: Use a plastic core instead of an iron core
explanation: Iron cores greatly enhance the strength of an electromagnet because iron is a ferromagnetic material that concentrates and amplifies the magnetic field. Plastic is non-magnetic and would not enhance the field, making it the only option that would NOT increase electromagnet strength. The other three options (more coils, increased current, and thicker wire) all genuinely increase electromagnet strength.
